name: Cambridge Centre for Ageing and Neuroscience (Cam-CAN)
description: A cross-sectional, population-based adult lifespan sample (~700 participants aged 18–87) with multimodal MRI, MEG, and cognitive-behavioral data spanning attention, emotion, action, language and memory. Its emotion and face-processing tasks and rich lifespan coverage make it valuable for studying how social and affective processing changes with age.
citation: Taylor JR, Williams N, Cusack R, et al. (2017). The Cambridge Centre for Ageing and Neuroscience (Cam-CAN) data repository: Structural and functional MRI, MEG, and cognitive data from a cross-sectional adult lifespan sample. NeuroImage, 144, 262–269.

publications.title: The Cam-CAN study protocol: a cross-sectional, lifespan, multidisciplinary examination of healthy cognitive ageing
